differential_diagnoses: High-grade lymphoma (e.g., Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma)
Metastatic carcinoma (e.g., HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma)
Vascular neoplasm (e.g., epithelioid hemangioendothelioma or angiosarcoma)
next_steps: Perform contrast-enhanced MRI of the neck to better characterize soft tissue margins and relationship to major vessels.
Consult interventional radiology for pre-biopsy assessment of vascularity and potential for selective embolization.
Obtain definitive tissue biopsy in a controlled operating room setting with immediate surgical/vascular backup.
Perform urgent histopathological evaluation with immunohistochemistry (desmin, myogenin, MyoD1, CD20, CD3, AE1/AE3).
Order systemic staging (PET/CT) to rule out metastatic disease prior to definitive surgical or oncological treatment.
Order baseline laboratory workup including CBC, metabolic profile, and LDH.
